title: polemonium viscosum subsp. eximium
intro: Polemonium viscosum subsp. eximium, also known as eximium Jacob's-ladder, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae, native to the mountainous regions of North America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Polemonium viscosum subsp. eximium include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
